name: NetSuite REST API
what_it_does: Oracle NetSuite's REST and SOAP APIs for accessing financial data, inventory, CRM, and operational business data from NetSuite's cloud ERP platform.
use_cases: Querying financial records (invoices, bills, journal entries) from NetSuite
Syncing NetSuite customers and contacts with external CRMs
Pulling inventory and warehouse data for fulfillment automation
Integrating NetSuite financial data into BI and reporting tools
not_for: Small businesses not in Oracle NetSuite ecosystem
Real-time low-latency operations (NetSuite API can be slow)
best_when: Your business runs on NetSuite and you need to integrate ERP data with external systems or build custom automation.
avoid_when: You're not using NetSuite or need a modern, developer-friendly ERP integration.

auth.notes: OAuth 1.0a (legacy TBA - Token Based Authentication) or OAuth 2.0 (newer, preferred). Account-specific domain required (ACCOUNT_ID.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com). Setup requires NetSuite admin.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; dev sandbox with paid subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Mid-market: $999/month+; Enterprise: custom pricing via Oracle sales
pricing.notes: Enterprise ERP pricing — typically $1,000-$10,000+/month depending on modules and users. API access included with subscription. No self-serve pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrency and request limits based on account tier; typically 10 concurrent requests

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages can be verbose but often reference internal NetSuite codes. SuiteAnalytics errors are particularly cryptic. SOAP faults are XML-heavy.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Upsert operations available via externalId field. Standard creates are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Account-specific domain required (format: ACCOUNT_ID.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com) — no global endpoint
OAuth 1.0a (TBA) is complex to implement correctly — OAuth 2.0 is simpler but requires newer NetSuite version
NetSuite concurrency limits can reject requests during peak hours — implement backoff
Record types and field names are NetSuite-specific — requires ERP domain knowledge
SuiteScript (JavaScript in NetSuite) often easier for complex operations than REST API
REST API and SOAP API (SuiteTalk) have different capabilities — check which has the feature you need
Saved Searches provide powerful queries but require NetSuite admin to set up

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
